question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be above $98,000 on October 23?

Description: This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title has a final “Close” price higher than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with “1m” and “Candles” selected on the top bar.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

Price precision is determined by the number of decimal places in the source.

evidences.title: Bitcoin Q1 2025 Institutional Adoption and Market Analysis
evidences.content: Q2 2025 Outlook Macro Expectations: Q2 2025 is poised to test whether Bitcoin's bull thesis holds amid evolving macro conditions. Many analysts anticipate that global central banks may start easing monetary policy by mid-2025 if inflation cools – a potential tailwind for Bitcoin if liquidity conditions improve. The U.S. Fed's tone in Q2 (pause vs. hike) will be pivotal; any hint at rate cuts could renew risk-on appetite. Conversely, persistent inflation or economic shocks (e.g., an escalation in geopolitical tensions or higher energy prices) could delay a crypto market rebound. Investors will also watch equity correlations – Bitcoin's correlation with tech stocks spiked during Q1's turmoil, so any stabilization in equities could bolster crypto sentiment. Regulatory Events to Watch: [...] Switzerland and other European jurisdictions with clear crypto frameworks continued to attract institutional businesses such as crypto banks and exchange-traded products. [...] Bottom line Overall, Q1 2025 reinforced Bitcoin’s growing stature among institutions, even as markets underwent a healthy correction. Global regulatory progress and infrastructure maturation provided a stronger foundation...

evidences.title: Bitcoin's Price Prediction For 2025: What To Watch in October - Forbes
evidences.content: ### Bearish Projections Bearish analysts caution against over-exuberance, highlighting macroeconomic risks like tightening monetary policies and potential regulatory crackdowns. A historical correlation with M2 suggests that bitcoin may depreciate as monetary conditions tighten. Peter L. Brandt, a respected market analyst, warns that his technical analysis might indicate a crash to $78,000, but that the price could then either continue declining or rise precipitously. [...] ### Bullish Projections Bullish predictions for bitcoin's price in 2025 from posts on X suggest a significant upward trend. CNBC analyst Tom Lee’s target number is $250,000. Matthew Sigel of VanEck forecasts $180,000. These forecasts reflect a strong belief in continued institutional adoption, the impact of bitcoin ETFs, and the cyclical nature of bitcoin's price movements post-halving. Of course, mega-bulls like Cathie Wood and Michael Saylor expect multi-million dollar bitcoin in the next few years, though not necessarily in 2025. ### Bearish Projections [...] ### Macro-Economic Factors Macroeconomic conditions remain a significant factor influencing bitcoin’s performance. evidences.title: Bitcoin ETF Flows Turn Positive as BlackRock's IBIT Hits Top 1%
evidences.content: Bloomberg ETF analyst Eric Balchunas highlighted the turnaround, noting that BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) has pulled in $3 billion in recent flows. That figure places IBIT in the top 1% of all ETFs by flow volume. > "Bitcoin ETFs flows are back in the high life.. every single rolling period we track is now positive, haven't seen that in months," Balchunas stated. SoSoValue data shows the 12 spot Bitcoin ETFs recorded $335.8 million in net inflows on April 22 alone. IBIT accounted for $246.9 million of that total. Fidelity's FBTC added $56.7 million, while Bitwise's BITB contributed $15.4 million. Bitcoin ETF Flows Bitcoin ETF Flows. Source: SoSoValue [...] ... crypto background image BeInCrypto # Bitcoin ETF Flows Turn Positive as BlackRock’s IBIT Hits Top 1% Lockridge Okoth 2 min read BTC-USD +1.54% IBIT +3.56% SOSO-USD +4.68% US spot Bitcoin (BTC) ETF flows have turned positive across every rolling period tracked by analysts. It is the first time all periods have aligned in positive territory in months. The shift signals renewed institutional appetite as BTC trades near $77,700, with cumulative lifetime net inflows sitting at $62.8 billion. ## Bitcoin ETF Flows Tur...

evidences.title: Why bitcoin institutional demand is on the rise
evidences.content: Institutional demand for bitcoin has been supported by increased access through registered vehicles. For institutions, direct crypto ownership has historically involved fragmented custody, murky compliance, and operational complexity. Bitcoin remains the dominant digital asset and was introduced in 2008 by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto.

evidences.title: Bitcoin Spot-ETF Flow Race Dominated by Fidelity, BlackRock (FBTC, IBIT) - Bloomberg
evidences.content: ### For Customers ### Support Americas+1 212 318 2000 EMEA+44 20 7330 7500 Asia Pacific+65 6212 1000 Crypto ETFs: # Fidelity and BlackRock Are Dominating the Bitcoin-ETF Flow Race Less than two weeks after US regulators fired the starting gun for ten spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds, it’s increasingly looking like a two-horse race for new assets. BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (ticker IBIT) and the Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund (FBTC) are leading the field with about $1.9 billion and $1.6 billion of inflows, respectively, according the latest available data compiled by Bloomberg. That’s roughly a combined 70% of spot Bitcoin ETF inflows so farBloomberg Terminal. evidences.title: Crypto Market Trends: Bitcoin Price Forecasts and the Rise of ...
evidences.content: ## Future Outlook ### Predictions for 2025 and Beyond The future outlook for Bitcoin and the cryptocurrency market remains optimistic, with several analysts predicting significant price appreciation. According to a report by Fool.com, the price of Bitcoin could reach nearly $150,000 by 2025 in a base case scenario, driven by continued institutional investment and the approval of Bitcoin ETFs. In a bull case scenario, the price could potentially reach $1 million or more. Bernstein’s report predicts that Bitcoin will reach $200,000 in 2025, $500,000 by 2029, and $1 million by 2033, driven by further institutional adoption and ETF inflows. ### Factors Influencing Future Trends Several factors will influence future trends in the cryptocurrency market: [...] A report by broker Bernstein suggests that Bitcoin will reach a cycle high of $200,000 in 2025, $500,000 by 2029, and $1 million by 2033. These predictions are based on the assumption of continued institutional adoption and the approval of Bitcoin ETFs by major financial institutions. ### Factors Influencing Price Predictions Several factors influence Bitcoin price predictions, including:

evidences.title: Maximum Pain For Option Buyers Going Into Expiration
evidences.content: ## What is open interest and why does it matter? Open interest tells you the total size of any positions that are currently open for a given instrument, or group of instruments. It’s worth noting that open interest is not the same as volume, as it only includes positions that are still open. Any closed positions will still be included in volume figures but not included in OI figures. For the bitcoin options on Deribit, the OI is given in the number of contracts currently open. Paying attention to the OI figures for a particular instrument, and even an entire expiration date that encompasses many instruments, can be useful because it can show how much financial interest there is in the underlying price being at a certain point at a specific time (expiration). [...] The lower the time to expiry, the shorter the time any entity attempting to move the price would need to keep up their efforts, and therefore the more cost effective it may be. It is unlikely for example that anyone would find the idea of attempting to pin the price to a specific strike for a full month very attractive, just because the max pain happens to currently be there. Used by itself max pain is a limited tool,...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price - History, Chart, 5 Years, 10 Years, Halving
evidences.content: ## What is Bitcoin's all-time high price record? November 2021 saw Bitcoin's all-time high price record, around $69,000. This milestone captures the rising institutional interest in blockchain technology as well as their acceptance. But Bitcoin's volatility means its price might vary greatly, hence investors have to exercise great caution. The price swings of Bitcoin rely on several elements, including market mood, laws, and more general state of the economy. Investing in Bitcoin presents hazards even if it presents chances. Before investing, one should investigate carefully and get financial guidance. [...] Attracting the interest of mainstream investors, Bitcoin hit a then-all-time high in 2017 around $20,000. Its value had half by early 2018, though, which cast questions about its long-term viability. Still, Bitcoin proved tough. It exceeded past milestones in 2020 and 2021, rising beyond $60,000. Macroeconomic issues including inflation, together with institutional interest, drove its comeback. These elements let investors view Bitcoin as a reasonable inflation hedge. [...] Combining speculative bubbles, outside pressure, and legislative changes usually produces price crashe...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Price Prediction 2025, 2026, 2030. Experts BTC Forecast ...
evidences.content: ### How often does Bitcoin hit new highs? Bitcoin often moves in 4-year cycles. Historically, BTC has reached a new high within 12–18 months post-halving. The current cycle, influenced by ETF inflows and tightening supply, could follow a similar trajectory—though external shocks could shift the timeline. ### Where can I view Bitcoin analytics? You can view bitcoin data on platforms like Glassnode, CryptoQuant, and CoinMetrics. These sites provide insight into on-chain activity, such as exchange flows, miner behavior, and long-term holder trends—all of which influence the bitcoin market outlook. [...] ### How often does Bitcoin hit new highs? Bitcoin often moves in 4-year cycles. Historically, BTC has reached a new high within 12–18 months post-halving. The current cycle, influenced by ETF inflows and tightening supply, could follow a similar trajectory—though external shocks could shift the timeline. ### Where can I view Bitcoin analytics?
